title: Define Structure Worksheet (Components)
text: Define Structure Worksheet (Components) Use the Define Structure worksheet to create or edit the component data structure and set the properties of the component, and its child objects. The component structure defines the component data and the way it is accessed and validated at runtime. For more information, see Component Data Structure . Tools Resource Browser—lists templates and modeled objects (entities and fields) that can be used to create component entities and fields in the component structure. Component Structure Editor—shows the objects in the component structure, and selected properties, as a tree. You can edit these properties here or in the Properties Inspector. Properties Inspector—shows the properties of the object selected in the Component Structure Editor, and enables you to edit them. How to Define the Component Structure Filter and Search for Objects in Resource Browser Using the Uniface IDE Working with Development Objects Related Links Component Data Structure Every component has a data structure that defines its component entities and fields, and the path required to access the data. The data structure affects the validation rules for data I/O, such as enforcing referential integrity constraints. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
